subject: [PATCH] userfaultfd: wait on source PMD during UFFDIO_MOVE

reviews.author: Andrew Morton
reviews.summary: Reviewer Andrew Morton added a cc:stable tag due to Sashiko's warning of potential wild spinlock acquisition and memory corruption, but did not provide specific technical details or suggestions for the patch itself.
reviews.raw_body:
Sashiko says "wild spinlock acquisition and memory corruption", so I'll
add a cc:stable to this.

2026-07-05.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the userfaultfd code where it incorrectly passes a pointer to src_pmdval instead of the actual PMD (page-table entry) when calling pmd_migration_entry_wait(). The fix involves passing the correct PMD, src_pmd, which matches the behavior in other parts of the kernel. This change ensures that the wait operation is properly synchronized with the real page-table lock, preventing potential issues on split-PMD-lock kernels.

reviews.author: Lance Yang
reviews.summary: reviewer noted that the patch should be sent to stable branch and gave a Reviewed-by tag
reviews.raw_body:
As Andrew and Baolin pointed out, this should go to stable ;)

With that,

Reviewed-by: Lance Yang <lance.yang@linux.dev>
